pattern: Equal averages force a subset whose sum is a fixed rational fraction of the total, reducing the question to whether some size-k subset hits an exact target sum.
hints: If both parts share the average, that average equals the whole array's average, so a chosen subset of size k must sum to total*k/n.
Only sizes k where total*k is divisible by n can work; enumerate those sizes and ask whether any k-element subset reaches the required sum.
Build reachable (count, sum) states with a DP set, or split the array in half and meet in the middle, to test subset-sum feasibility for each viable k without trying all subsets blindly.

edge_inputs.description: two elements cannot be split into two non-empty equal-average parts
single element cannot be split at all
average not realizable by any proper subset
presence of a zero with a matching complement
odd structure where no valid k divides evenly

stress.description: small arrays so the exponential brute oracle stays well under the time budget
stress.note: n capped at 16 because the oracle enumerates subsets
